prerequisites.title: Prerequisites
prerequisites.description: Requirements depend on your deployment method:
prerequisites.dockerCompose: Docker Compose: Docker and Docker Compose (PostgreSQL is included)
prerequisites.binary: Manual: PostgreSQL 15+ (no Docker, Node.js, or web server needed)
prerequisites.enterprise: Premium Features: Activate a license key in Settings → License (all methods)

dockerCompose.step2Note: This creates a .env file with secure random secrets (JWT_SECRET, ENCRYPTION_KEY, POSTGRES_PASSWORD).
dockerCompose.step6AlertWorker: Alert Worker: running in background — polls your RabbitMQ servers every 5 minutes and writes alerts to the database.

dokku.whyDokkuEasyDeployment: Easy deployment: Git push to deploy, like Heroku
dokku.whyDokkuAutoSsl: Automatic SSL: Let's Encrypt certificates out of the box
dokku.step1Description: See the <dokkuLink>Dokku Installation Guide</dokkuLink> for your platform.
dokku.step2Note: DATABASE_URL is automatically set by Dokku when you link the database.
dokku.step3Note: PORT and HOST are automatically set by Dokku. Replace https://your-domain.com with your actual domain.
dokku.step5WorkerNote: The worker process type is defined in the Procfile and runs the alert monitor. Dokku does not start it automatically — you must scale it to 1. Setting ALERT_WORKER_EMBEDDED=false prevents the web process from also running the cron.
dokku.step6Note: Your app will be available at https://your-domain.com (or your server's IP).

binary.description: Download a single binary — no Docker, Node.js, or Nginx required. Only PostgreSQL is needed. Windows users can run the Linux binary inside WSL2.
binary.postgresInstallTitle: Install PostgreSQL if you don't have it:
binary.dbSetupDescription: Create a dedicated PostgreSQL user and database for Qarote:
binary.step1Note: This downloads the latest release automatically. You can also <releasesLink>browse all releases</releasesLink> on GitHub.
binary.step2Note: The setup wizard will ask for your PostgreSQL URL, verify the connection, and generate a .env file with secure secrets.
binary.step3WorkerNote: The alert worker polls your RabbitMQ servers every 5 minutes and writes health alerts to the database. Run it in a separate terminal or as a background service.
binary.step4Description: Open http://localhost:3000 in your browser. The binary serves both the API and frontend from a single port.
binary.step4ManualSetup: Manual setup: You can also skip the wizard and configure directly:
binary.setupWizardTitle: The setup wizard will ask you to:
binary.setupWizardCreateAdmin: Create an admin account (recommended) — a pre-created user so you can log in immediately without signing up
binary.setupWizardConfigureRegistration: Configure public registration — whether anyone can sign up via the web form
binary.setupWizardTip: Tip: For a secure setup, say Yes to admin account and No to public registration. You can invite team members later via invite links.

smtp.title: SMTP Configuration
smtp.description: Email features are disabled by default for self-hosted deployments. To enable email (for password resets, invitations, notifications), configure SMTP settings.
smtp.oauth2Description: For production environments, OAuth2 is the recommended authentication method. It's more secure than app passwords and doesn't require storing credentials directly.
smtp.oauth2Benefit2: Tokens can be revoked without changing passwords
smtp.oauth2Benefit3: Better for enterprise and high-volume sending
smtp.oauth2DocsLink: For detailed OAuth2 setup instructions, see the <nodemailerLink>Nodemailer OAuth2 Documentation</nodemailerLink>. Examples for Gmail and Office 365 are shown below.
smtp.gmailAppPasswordReq2: Generate an <appPasswordLink>App Password</appPasswordLink> (not your regular password)
smtp.gmailAppPasswordReq3: Sending limits: 500 emails/day for free Gmail accounts, 2,000/day for Google Workspace
smtp.gmailOauth2Step2: Create a new project (or use existing)
smtp.gmailOauth2Step3: Enable Gmail API for your project
smtp.gmailOauth2Step4: Create OAuth 2.0 credentials (OAuth client ID → Web application)
smtp.gmailOauth2Step6: Use <oauthPlaygroundLink>OAuth2 Playground</oauthPlaygroundLink> to get refresh token:
smtp.gmailOauth2Step6Sub1: Click gear icon → Use your own OAuth credentials
smtp.gmailOauth2Step6Sub2: Enter Client ID and Client Secret
smtp.gmailOauth2DetailedInstructions: For detailed instructions, see <nodemailerGmailLink>Nodemailer Gmail OAuth2 Guide</nodemailerGmailLink>
smtp.sendgridReq2: Password is your SendGrid API key (create at Settings → API Keys)
smtp.sendgridReq3: Verify your sender domain in SendGrid dashboard first
smtp.mailgunReq1: Add and verify your domain in Mailgun dashboard first
smtp.mailgunReq3: Find SMTP credentials in Mailgun → Sending → Domain settings
smtp.mailgunReq4: Free tier: First 3 months free (up to 5,000/month), then paid only
smtp.amazonSesReq2: SMTP credentials are not your AWS access keys — generate them in SES console → SMTP Settings
smtp.amazonSesReq3: Verify your sender email or domain in SES before sending
smtp.amazonSesReq4: New accounts start in sandbox mode (can only send to verified addresses) — request production access to remove limits
smtp.amazonSesReq5: Free tier: 62,000 emails/month when sent from an EC2 instance, otherwise $0.10 per 1,000 emails
smtp.office365BasicImportant: Important: Office 365 requires Modern Authentication. You must enable SMTP AUTH in your Microsoft 365 admin center:
smtp.office365BasicReq1: Go to Microsoft 365 admin center → Settings → Org settings
smtp.office365BasicReq2: Select "Modern authentication" and enable SMTP AUTH
smtp.office365BasicReq3: If using 2FA/MFA, generate an App Password instead of your regular password
smtp.office365Oauth2Description: OAuth2 is recommended for Office 365/Outlook as it doesn't require enabling legacy SMTP AUTH.
smtp.office365Oauth2DocsLink: For detailed Microsoft 365 OAuth2 setup instructions, see the <nodemailerLink>Nodemailer OAuth2 Documentation</nodemailerLink>.
smtp.additionalOauth2Description: For OAuth2 configuration with other providers (Outlook.com, Yahoo, AOL, etc.), refer to the comprehensive <nodemailerLink>Nodemailer OAuth2 Documentation</nodemailerLink>. It includes:
smtp.additionalOauth2Item1: Step-by-step setup guides for major providers
smtp.additionalOauth2Item2: Code examples for token generation and refresh
smtp.testingDescription: Use the built-in SMTP testing script to verify your configuration:
smtp.testingEtherealNote: Ethereal Email is a fake SMTP service — test emails are never delivered but viewable via a web preview URL.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.execFormatDescription: This means you downloaded the wrong architecture. Check your platform with uname -m (x86_64 = x64, aarch64 = ARM64), then re-download the correct binary in-place:
troubleshooting.execFormatTip: Multipass on Apple Silicon creates ARM64 VMs — use Linux ARM64, not Linux x64.

binary.wontStartItem1: Check the terminal output for error messages
binary.wontStartItem2: Verify your .env file exists or pass all required CLI flags
binary.databaseItem1: Verify your DATABASE_URL is correct and PostgreSQL is running
binary.databaseItem3: Migrations run automatically on startup — check the output for errors

dockerCompose.wontStartItem3: Ensure ports are available (or change BACKEND_PORT / FRONTEND_PORT in .env)
dockerCompose.databaseItem1: Wait for PostgreSQL to fully initialize (check health status)
dockerCompose.databaseItem3: Run migrations: docker exec qarote_backend pnpm run db:migrate

dokku.wontStartItem3: Ensure the app was deployed: dokku ps:report qarote
dokku.databaseItem1: Verify the database is linked: dokku postgres:info qarote-db
dokku.databaseItem2: Check that DATABASE_URL is set: dokku config:get qarote DATABASE_URL
dokku.databaseItem3: Migrations run automatically on deploy — check logs for errors

troubleshooting.licenseItem1: Verify your license key is valid and not expired
troubleshooting.licenseItem2: Re-activate your license from Settings → License

security.title: Security Recommendations
security.item1: Use strong, randomly generated values for JWT_SECRET and ENCRYPTION_KEY if not using the setup-generated defaults
security.item2: Keep your license key private — do not share it publicly
security.item3: Regularly update to the latest version

dockerCompose.description: Production-ready Docker Compose configuration for self-hosted deployments. Not needed for binary or Dokku deployments.

envConfig.description: Environment variables reference for Docker Compose and Dokku deployments. Manual install users should run ./qarote setup instead.
